channel.title: Docker.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Docker.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Docker AI Governance: Unlock Agent Autonomy, Safely
item.description: Introducing Docker AI Governance: centralized control over how agents execute, what they can reach on the network, which credentials they can use, and which MCP tools they can call, so every developer in your company can run AI agents safely, wherever they work. Your laptop is the new prod Agents are the biggest productivity unlock... Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/docker-ai-governance-unlock-agent-autonomy-safely/

item.title: Comparing Different Approaches to Sandboxing
item.description: Whether you are a software engineer, a product manager, or a designer, this quote should fundamentally change how we approach our daily routine. We are no longer just building interfaces; we are creating environments where agents can operate autonomously with minimal human interaction. What could be the fundamental requirement for such an environment ? In... Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/comparing-sandboxing-approaches-ai-agents/

item.title: Generate Images Locally with Docker Model Runner and Open WebUI
item.description: We've all been there: you need to generate a few images for a project, you fire up an AI image service, and suddenly you're wondering what happens to your prompts, how many credits you have left, or why that "safe content" filter rejected your perfectly reasonable request for a dragon wearing a business suit. What... Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/blog-generate-images-locally-dmr-open-webui/

item.title: Precision Container Security with Docker and Black Duck
item.description: The complexity of modern containerized applications often leaves developers drowning in a sea of "noise"—vulnerabilities that exist in the file system but pose zero actual risk to the application. The integration between Black Duck and Docker Hardened Images (DHI) provides a definitive answer to this challenge. By combining Docker’s secure-by-default foundations, using VEX (Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange)... Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/precision-container-security-with-docker-and-black-duck/
